Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n3: \\n4: package endpoint\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n9: \\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/cilium/hive/cell\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n12: \\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/clustermesh\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/clustermesh/wait\\\"\\n15: \\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n16: \\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time\\\"\\n17: )\\n18: \\n19: var (\\n20: \\tRegeneratorCell = cell.Module(\\n21: \\t\\t\\\"endpoint-regeneration\\\",\\n22: \\t\\t\\\"Endpoints regeneration\\\",\\n23: \\n24: \\t\\tcell.Provide(newRegenerator),\\n25: \\t)\\n26: )\\n27: \\n28: // KVStoreNodesWaitFn is the type of the function used to wait for synchronization\\n29: // of all nodes from the kvstore.\\n30: type KVStoreNodesWaitFn wait.Fn\\n31: \\n32: // Regenerator wraps additional functionalities for endpoint regeneration.\\n33: type Regenerator struct {\\n34: \\tnodesWaitFn   KVStoreNodesWaitFn\\n35: \\tcmWaitFn      wait.Fn\\n36: \\tcmWaitTimeout time.Duration\\n37: \\n38: \\tlogger        logrus.FieldLogger\\n39: \\tcmSyncLogOnce sync.Once\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: func newRegenerator(in struct {\\n43: \\tcell.In\\n44: \\n45: \\tLogger logrus.FieldLogger\\n46: \\n47: \\tConfig      wait.TimeoutConfig\\n48: \\tNodesWaitFn KVStoreNodesWaitFn\\n49: \\tClusterMesh *clustermesh.ClusterMesh\\n50: }) *Regenerator {\\n51: \\twaitFn := func(context.Context) error { return nil }\\n52: \\tif in.ClusterMesh != nil {\\n53: \\t\\twaitFn = in.ClusterMesh.IPIdentitiesSynced\\n54: \\t}\\n55: \\n56: \\treturn &Regenerator{\\n57: \\t\\tlogger:        in.Logger,\\n58: \\t\\tnodesWaitFn:   in.NodesWaitFn,\\n59: \\t\\tcmWaitFn:      waitFn,\\n60: \\t\\tcmWaitTimeout: in.Config.Timeout(),\\n61: \\t}\\n62: }\\n63: \\n64: // CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration caps the timeout to a value suitable in\\n65: // case the regeneration of an endpoint needs to be performed synchronously\\n66: // (currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\\n67: // to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\\n68: // workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\\n69: // forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\\n70: func (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\\n71: \\tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\\n72: \\tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\\n73: \\t\\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\\n74: \\t\\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\\n75: \\t\\t\\tInfo(\\\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\\\")\\n76: \\t}\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\\n80: \\treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\\n81: }\\n82: \\n83: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\\n84: \\twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\\n85: \\tdefer cancel()\\n86: \\terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\\n87: \\n88: \\tswitch {\\n89: \\tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\\n90: \\t\\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\\n91: \\t\\treturn ErrNotAlive\\n92: \\tcase err != nil:\\n93: \\t\\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\\n94: \\t\\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\\n95: \\t\\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\\n96: \\t\\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\\n97: \\t\\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\\n98: \\t\\t\\tr.logger.Warning(\\\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\\\")\\n99: \\t\\t})\\n100: \\t}\\n101: \\n102: \\treturn nil\\n103: }\\n104: \t\t\"Endpoints regeneration\",\n23: \n24: \t\tcell.Provide(newRegenerator),\n25: \t)\n26: )\n27: \n28: // KVStoreNodesWaitFn is the type of the function used to wait for synchronization\n29: // of all nodes from the kvstore.\n30: type KVStoreNodesWaitFn wait.Fn\n31: \n32: // Regenerator wraps additional functionalities for endpoint regeneration.\n33: type Regenerator struct {\n34: \tnodesWaitFn   KVStoreNodesWaitFn\n35: \tcmWaitFn      wait.Fn\n36: \tcmWaitTimeout time.Duration\n37: \n38: \tlogger        logrus.FieldLogger\n39: \tcmSyncLogOnce sync.Once\n40: }\n41: \n42: func newRegenerator(in struct {\n43: \tcell.In\n44: \n45: \tLogger logrus.FieldLogger\n46: \n47: \tConfig      wait.TimeoutConfig\n48: \tNodesWaitFn KVStoreNodesWaitFn\n49: \tClusterMesh *clustermesh.ClusterMesh\n50: }) *Regenerator {\n51: \twaitFn := func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n52: \tif in.ClusterMesh != nil {\n53: \t\twaitFn = in.ClusterMesh.IPIdentitiesSynced\n54: \t}\n55: \n56: \treturn &Regenerator{\n57: \t\tlogger:        in.Logger,\n58: \t\tnodesWaitFn:   in.NodesWaitFn,\n59: \t\tcmWaitFn:      waitFn,\n60: \t\tcmWaitTimeout: in.Config.Timeout(),\n61: \t}\n62: }\n63: \n64: // CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration caps the timeout to a value suitable in\n65: // case the regeneration of an endpoint needs to be performed synchronously\n66: // (currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\n67: // to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\n68: // workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\n69: // forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\n70: func (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\n71: \tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n72: \tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\n73: \t\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\n74: \t\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\n75: \t\t\tInfo(\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\")\n76: \t}\n77: }\n78: \n79: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n80: \treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\n81: }\n82: \n83: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n84: \twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\n85: \tdefer cancel()\n86: \terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\n87: \n88: \tswitch {\n89: \tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n90: \t\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\n91: \t\treturn ErrNotAlive\n92: \tcase err != nil:\n93: \t\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\n94: \t\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\n95: \t\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\n96: \t\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\n97: \t\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\n98: \t\t\tr.logger.Warning(\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\")\n99: \t\t})\n100: \t}\n101: \n102: \treturn nil\n103: }\n104: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cilium--v1.16.5@14bf8b70da6d30733726dd61ffc026d1ed2b187c/-/blob/pkg/endpoint/regenerator.go"}}}

caps the timeout to a value suitable in\n    65→// case the regeneration of an endpoint needs to be performed synchronously\n    66→// (currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\n    67→// to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\n    68→// workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\n    69→// forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\n    70→func (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\n    71→\tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n    72→\tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\n    73→\t\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\n    74→\t\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\n    75→\t\t\tInfo(\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\")\n    76→\t}\n    77→}\n    78→\n    79→func (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n    80→\treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\n    81→}\n    82→\n    83→func (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n    84→\twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\n    85→\tdefer cancel()\n    86→\terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\n    87→\n    88→\tswitch {\n    89→\tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n    90→\t\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\n    91→\t\treturn ErrNotAlive\n    92→\tcase err != nil:\n    93→\t\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\n    94→\t\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\n    95→\t\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\n    96→\t\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\n    97→\t\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\n    98→\t\t\tr.logger.Warning(\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\")\n    99→\t\t})\n   100→\t}\n   101→\n   102→\treturn nil\n   103→}\n   104→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage endpoint\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/hive/cell\"\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/clustermesh\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/clustermesh/wait\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tRegeneratorCell = cell.Module(\n\t\t\"endpoint-regeneration\",\n\t\t\"Endpoints regeneration\",\n\n\t\tcell.Provide(newRegenerator),\n\t)\n)\n\n// KVStoreNodesWaitFn is the type of the function used to wait for synchronization\n// of all nodes from the kvstore.\ntype KVStoreNodesWaitFn wait.Fn\n\n// Regenerator wraps additional functionalities for endpoint regeneration.\ntype Regenerator struct {\n\tnodesWaitFn   KVStoreNodesWaitFn\n\tcmWaitFn      wait.Fn\n\tcmWaitTimeout time.Duration\n\n\tlogger        logrus.FieldLogger\n\tcmSyncLogOnce sync.Once\n}\n\nfunc newRegenerator(in struct {\n\tcell.In\n\n\tLogger logrus.FieldLogger\n\n\tConfig      wait.TimeoutConfig\n\tNodesWaitFn KVStoreNodesWaitFn\n\tClusterMesh *clustermesh.ClusterMesh\n}) *Regenerator {\n\twaitFn := func(context.Context) error { return nil }\n\tif in.ClusterMesh != nil {\n\t\twaitFn = in.ClusterMesh.IPIdentitiesSynced\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Regenerator{\n\t\tlogger:        in.Logger,\n\t\tnodesWaitFn:   in.NodesWaitFn,\n\t\tcmWaitFn:      waitFn,\n\t\tcmWaitTimeout: in.Config.Timeout(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration caps the timeout to a value suitable in\n// case the regeneration of an endpoint needs to be performed synchronously\n// (currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\n// to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\n// workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\n// forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\nfunc (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\n\tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n\tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\n\t\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\n\t\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\n\t\t\tInfo(\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n\treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n\twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n\t\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\n\t\treturn ErrNotAlive\n\tcase err != nil:\n\t\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\n\t\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\n\t\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\n\t\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\n\t\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\t\tr.logger.Warning(\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n","numLines":104,"startLine":1,"totalLines":104}}}
